note: Fathom meeting transcripts + distilled context Alex pastes in. Each entry: date, title, attendees, tags, summary, and notes (markdown). Used as context for ads, offers, messaging. Speaker diarization is unreliable — store team CONCLUSIONS, not who-said-which-quote.

meetings.title: Team strategy — Lucy/Keith/Tyson ads, DM accountability, incentives
meetings.creator: Alex · Matt · Will · (3h)
meetings.summary: Re-targeted every creator's ICP, designed a DM follow-up accountability system, validated the Sales Hub numbers, and landed on a focused weekend plan: push Lucy only, hold Tyson + Keith until Sunday.
meetings.notes:
## The plan they landed on (this week)
*(Final order of operations — this SUPERSEDED an earlier idea in the call to double Tyson + route 20% to Kels; the weekend-calendar reasoning below changed the call.)*

1. **Lucy is priority #1.** Launch new Lucy direct-CTA ads — 5 angles (below) in ONE ad set, let the algorithm spend, then force spend to the winner. Ramp to **$300/day** (not $500 yet — too new). Run it through the weekend (2–3 days), watch **day-over-day** + decide on **7 days**.
2. **Will's only job this week:** be in Lucy's DMs, make sure **Debbie** is dialed, sales team on point. If sales is solid, Alex is freed to execute ads.
3. **Do NOT touch Tyson or Keith spend until Sunday.** Reason: weekend calendar. Upping spend Fri books calls into a weekend with no closer coverage. Sunday → raise Tyson + Keith.
4. **Install the next closer for next weekend** (solves the weekend-availability hole). Will scheduling interviews.
5. **Keith:** change nothing yet — Matt's Keith data isn't dialed; Alex + Matt review Keith numbers together, report to Will.

## Current read per creator (ICP + ad angles)
- **Tyson** — the proven engine. Alpha-male voice, direct-CTA working (~4x). Note: team feels his real persona is softer than the alpha positioning, which may cap it.
- **Keith** — current 'high-value' targeting is WRONG. Real audience: younger, **mostly females love him**; the males are beginners/lost-their-gym-support-group, seeking community. New direction: emotional + beginner + community ('feel lost in the gym', 'lost your support system', 'going but not seeing results'). Data confirms: lead-magnet works (~6–7x), direct-CTA failing (~0.7–1.5x) → fix the messaging, keep the lead magnet.
- **Lucy** — NOT 'female Tyson'. ICP: Whole Foods + faith + lifting + running, looks great, 25, herniated-disc injury → lost her physique → faith/gratitude origin. **5 ad angles to test (direct CTA):** (1) Food — whole foods vs 'health foods', 'eating right but not seeing results'; (2) Running — runners are lean-but-soft, 'running to lose fat but looking worse'; (3) Faith — temple/God's design + whole foods (ONLY with Lucy's approval, handle with care); (4) Weight training — women's fear of getting bulky / don't know how to start; (5) Her story — injury → lost it all → found herself (God + self-accountability) → coaching. Female pain points: bloating, energy, PCOS, endometriosis, hormones, the 3-good-weeks/1-bad-week cycle. Copy: short (3rd–5th grade reading, 2–3s attention), CTA leaves suspense.
- **Antoine** — onboards in ~10 days. ICP: older alpha males like Tyson, 150–200k+ earners / CEO-level / family, 'lost everything physically, have everything materially'. Soften his hard IFBB-pro look; 'anomaly' angle ('I'm top 1% genetics — you can be top 10%'). Alex finds him easiest (Tyson-like voice).

## Operational builds decided
- **'Go Hunt / Time to Eat' DM accountability system (Matt to build):** if a lead replies and isn't answered within ~24h → tag it → a centralized **DMs tab** (new standalone tab in the app) shows 'new opportunity' deep-links; opening one reassigns the lead to whoever grabs it (ManyChat reassigns on reply). 2nd offense → 'dead meat' for accountability. Needs: all setters on all accounts (+~$300–500/mo ManyChat seats), stored ManyChat user IDs, and **response-time tracking (11am–11pm window)** per company/client/setter.
- **Setter inbox fix:** Gideon (Keith) has 383 open convos = disorganized, dropping even 2-day-old leads. Rule: close anything >5 days. Follow-up VA trained Sun, live Mon.
- **Kels = floater** across all 3 accounts (~50 new leads/day, ~20% of direct-CTA), since Amara is bandwidth-capped on Tyson CTA and is the strongest setter; Kels is the test case for the new setup.

## Strategy / doctrine in action
- **More → Better → New, live:** a 'push straight to checkout / free-community' idea was correctly judged 'a lot of new' and shelved — do the simplest thing first (just change Lucy/Keith targeting inside the existing DM funnel), revisit new in 2–3 weeks if still believed.
- **The constraint right now = setter time/bandwidth** (not budget). Every ads decision (direct-CTA vs lead-magnet, how much to spend) gates on it.
- **Ad-testing method:** many angles × 3–5 creatives, ~$100–150/day test, REMOVE losers (don't just keep winners), force spend to winners, decide every 5–7 days.
- **Business model:** influencers pay nothing upfront; CCOS fronts ad spend, invoices expenses, splits profit 50/50 (keeps more for the risk). Extremely sticky → near-zero churn. Debated a 15–100k setup fee, decided getting them in beats the upfront cash.
- **Incentives (Will to propose):** weekly + monthly closer competitions, team + individual, promote-from-within ladder (assistant sales mgr). Records: Will's best day $9,997 (Feb 4); 2026 avg week ≈ $17.7k cash. Will wants a partner path.

## My independent read / flags
- The Keith conclusion (lead-magnet works, fix messaging, hold) lines up with my earlier kill-the-cold-direct-CTA analysis — consistent.
- **The 5 Lucy angles are HYPOTHESES, not proven** — they come from Will's sales experience + 'the boys agree' + a few closed females, which Alex explicitly pushed on for statistical significance. The plan correctly treats them as a test; judge on real cost-per-lead / cost-per-call over 7 days.
- The Sales Hub show-rate was validated in the call (the 58% vs 72% gap was a word-miscalc bug; manual math = 53.57% matched the dashboard). Good — the numbers source is trustworthy, with that one fix noted.
